title: Order of 25 February 2026 — Embassy of Mexico in Quito (Mexico v. Ecuador) (Fixing of time-limits: Reply and Rejoinder)
text:
25 FEBRUARY 2026 
 
ORDER 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
EMBASSY OF MEXICO IN QUITO 
 
(MEXICO v. ECUADOR) 
 
 
 
 
 
 
___________ 
 
 
 
 
 
 
AMBASSADE DU MEXIQUE À QUITO 
 
(MEXIQUE c. ÉQUATEUR) 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
25 FÉVRIER 2026 
 
ORDONNANCE 

 
 
 
 
 
 
INTERNATIONAL COURT OF JUSTICE 
 
 
 
YEAR 2026 
 
2026 
 
25 February 
 
General List 
 
No. 194 
25 February 2026 
 
 
 
EMBASSY OF MEXICO IN QUITO 
 
(MEXICO v. ECUADOR) 
 
 
 
ORDER 
 
 
 
Present:  President IWASAWA; Vice-President SEBUTINDE; Judges TOMKA, ABRAHAM, XUE, 
NOLTE, CHARLESWORTH, BRANT, GÓMEZ ROBLEDO, CLEVELAND, AURESCU, TLADI, 
HMOUD, OKOWA; Judge ad hoc MCRAE; Registrar GAUTIER. 
 
 
 
The International Court of Justice,  
 
Composed as above,  
 
After deliberation, 
 
Having regard to Article 48 of the Statute of the Court and to Articles 31, 44, 45, paragraph 2, 
and 48 of the Rules of Court, 
 
Having regard to the Application filed in the Registry of the Court on 11 April 2024, whereby 
the United Mexican States (hereinafter “Mexico”) instituted proceedings against the Republic of 
Ecuador (hereinafter “Ecuador”) with respect to “legal questions concerning the settlement of 
international disputes by peaceful means and diplomatic relations, and the inviolability of a 
diplomatic mission”, 
 
 

- 2 - 
 
Having regard to the Order of 19 July 2024, whereby the Court fixed 22 April 2025 and 
22 January 2026 as the respective time-limits for the filing of a Memorial by Mexico and a 
Counter-Memorial by Ecuador, 
 
Having regard to the Memorial of Mexico and the Counter-Memorial of Ecuador filed within 
the time-limits thus fixed; 
 
Whereas, on 11 February 2026, a meeting was held by the President of the Court with the 
Agents and representatives of the Parties pursuant to Article 31 of the Rules of Court, in order to 
ascertain their views on the subsequent procedure in the case;  
 
Whereas, at the meeting, counsel for Mexico expressed the opinion that a second round of 
written pleadings would be of benefit to the Court; whereas he further indicated that the Government 
of Mexico wished the case to be resolved as expeditiously as possible and that, given the 
non-voluminous nature of the first round of written pleadings, a short period of time would suffice 
for the preparation of a Reply and a Rejoinder; whereas counsel for Mexico therefore proposed that 
the Court accord each Party a period of four months for the filing of its respective pleading; whereas 
the Co-Agent of Ecuador stated that his Government did not object to a second round of written 
pleadings; and whereas the Co-Agent observed that Ecuador would require a period of eight months 
for the filing of its Rejoinder; 
 
Whereas counsel for Mexico recalled that the Court had previously granted nine months for 
the preparation of the Memorial and the Counter‑Memorial, and noted that the Court generally fixed 
much shorter time-limits for the filing of a second round of written pleadings compared to the first 
round; whereas he emphasized that the Government of Mexico was willing to show flexibility with 
a view to reaching an understanding with Ecuador, but that a period of eight months would be 
excessive; and whereas counsel for Ecuador indicated that Ecuador could accept a time‑limit of six 
months for the filing of its Rejoinder; 
 
Whereas counsel for Mexico stated that, while his Government remained willing to reach an 
agreement with Ecuador concerning the length of the time‑limits, it was of the opinion that a period 
of time in excess of five months would not be appropriate; 
 
Taking into account the views of the Parties, 
 
Authorizes the submission of a Reply by the United Mexican States and a Rejoinder by the 
Republic of Ecuador; 
 
Fixes the following time-limits for the filing of the written pleadings: 
 
25 August 2026 for the Reply of the United Mexican States; 
 
25 February 2027 for the Rejoinder of the Republic of Ecuador; and 
 
Reserves the subsequent procedure for further decision. 
 
 
 
 

- 3 - 
 
Done in English and in French, the English text being authoritative, at the Peace Palace, 
The Hague, this twenty-fifth day of February, two thousand and twenty-six, in three copies, one of 
which will be placed in the archives of the Court and the others transmitted to the Government of the 
United Mexican States and the Government of the Republic of Ecuador, respectively. 
 
 
 
 
 
 
(Signed) 
IWASAWA Yuji, 
 
President. 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
(Signed) 
Philippe GAUTIER, 
 
Registrar. 
 
 
 
___________
case_name: Embassy of Mexico in Quito (Mexico v. Ecuador)
